wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for KatalogThrift, a multi-mitra thrift fashion marketplace. Explain the project's purpose as a Laravel-based platform connecting individual thrift stores with fashion-conscious consumers. Document prerequisites including PHP 8.1+, Composer, Node.js, and MySQL. Provide step-by-step installation instructions covering environment setup, dependency installation via Composer and npm, database configuration, migration execution, and development server startup. Include initial project structure overview, key configuration files, and basic usage examples. Address common setup issues and provide verification steps to ensure successful installation. Make content accessible to developers new to Laravel while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced PHP developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: MVC Pattern Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Laravel's MVC pattern implementation in KatalogThrift. Explain how the Model-View-Controller architecture separates concerns with detailed examples from the codebase. Document the Controller layer including base controller class, role-specific controllers (Member, Partner, Admin), and API controllers. Detail the Model layer with Eloquent ORM relationships, database migrations, and model factories. Explain the View layer using Blade templates with layout inheritance, component partials, and data binding. Include practical examples showing request flow from controller to model to view, error handling patterns, and data validation approaches. Address controller composition, service injection, and view rendering optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Guards and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the authentication guards configuration in KatalogThrift's multi-user system. Document the web and partner authentication guards setup, explaining how each guard uses session driver with Eloquent user provider. Detail the guard switching mechanism between Member and Partner roles, session management differences, and provider configuration for user authentication. Include practical examples of guard usage in controllers, middleware implementation, and route protection. Explain the authentication defaults configuration, user provider setup, and how the system handles multiple authentication contexts. Address guard-specific session handling, cookie configuration, and authentication state management across different user types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core User Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the User model which serves as the foundation for KatalogThrift's multi-role authentication system. Detail the User model's attributes including role-based permissions (admin, partner, member), gamification system with points and tier progression, and authentication features. Document the relationship with Partner model through foreign key constraints, password hashing mechanisms, and email verification processes. Explain the role-based access control methods (isAdmin, isPartner, isMember), tier badge system with emoji representations, and point accumulation logic. Include examples of user registration workflows, role assignment during registration, and automatic tier upgrades based on point thresholds. Document the activity logging integration for tracking user actions and maintaining audit trails.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for product CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations in KatalogThrift. Detail the complete workflow for partners to add new products, including form validation, image upload, and metadata management. Document the product creation interface, required fields, optional features, and validation rules. Explain product editing capabilities, batch updates, and approval workflows. Cover product deletion procedures, soft deletes, and data cleanup processes. Include partner-specific product management screens, admin moderation interfaces, and quality control measures. Document API endpoints for product operations, request/response formats, and authentication requirements. Provide step-by-step examples of product creation, editing, and deletion processes with screenshots and code samples.

wiki_catalogs.name: Member Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for member profile management functionality. Detail profile creation, editing, and updating workflows including personal information management, avatar uploads, and privacy settings. Document profile completion requirements, verification processes, and account activation procedures. Explain profile visibility settings, public/private toggles, and social sharing controls. Include profile analytics, visitor tracking, and engagement metrics. Address profile security features, password management, and two-factor authentication. Document profile data validation, sanitization, and storage mechanisms. Provide examples of profile customization options, theme selection, and personal branding features. Include troubleshooting guides for common profile issues and data migration scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Partner Onboarding and Registration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for partner onboarding and registration processes. Document the complete partner registration workflow including initial sign-up forms, business verification procedures, and account approval processes. Explain partner authentication mechanisms, login procedures, and session management. Detail the partner verification steps, document submission requirements, and approval workflows. Include partner dashboard access controls, role-based permissions, and security measures. Document partner account setup procedures, profile completion requirements, and onboarding checklists. Address partner support during onboarding, common issues encountered, and resolution procedures. Provide step-by-step guides for new partner registration, verification processes, and account activation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the admin dashboard and analytics system. Document the dashboard overview showing key metrics including total partners, pending approvals, product counts, member statistics, review volumes, and pending reports. Explain analytics reporting features including top performing partners, popular products, tier distribution analysis, and system-wide performance metrics. Detail the data visualization components, real-time statistics, and business intelligence features. Include practical examples of dashboard navigation, metric interpretation, and trend analysis workflows. Document the integration between dashboard widgets and underlying data models, performance optimization for large datasets, and custom reporting capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Editorial Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for KatalogThrift's editorial content management system. Detail the article creation workflow including categories (mix-match, tips-perawatan, tren, panduan), publishing status management, and SEO optimization features. Document the admin interface for content editors with CRUD operations, draft management, and approval workflows. Explain the public-facing article listing and detail pages with pagination, filtering by category, and related content recommendations. Include media handling for article covers, content formatting standards, and metadata management. Provide practical examples of content creation procedures, moderation processes, and content scheduling. Address content performance optimization, caching strategies, and mobile-responsive design considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for authentication endpoints covering member registration, partner registration, admin authentication, and token management. Document POST endpoints for user registration with validation rules, password requirements, and email verification processes. Detail login/logout endpoints with Sanctum token generation and refresh mechanisms. Explain role-based authentication flows for members, partners, and administrators. Include request/response schemas for authentication payloads, error responses for invalid credentials, and token expiration handling. Provide examples of successful authentication flows, multi-factor authentication integration, and session management. Address security considerations including CSRF protection, rate limiting, and secure token storage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blade Templating System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for KatalogThrift's Blade templating system. Detail the template inheritance hierarchy, layout composition, and component patterns used throughout the application. Document the role-based view structure including member, partner, and admin templates. Explain Blade syntax, directives, loops, conditionals, and template sections. Cover partial templates, component composition, and reusable UI patterns. Include practical examples of layout inheritance, data passing between templates, and dynamic content rendering. Document view naming conventions, folder organization, and template discovery patterns. Address template caching, performance optimization, and debugging techniques for Blade templates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for KatalogThrift. Detail unit testing approaches using PHPUnit, including test case organization, mocking strategies, and assertion patterns. Document feature testing methodologies for end-to-end functionality validation. Explain test database setup, factory usage, and seeder integration. Include testing best practices for Laravel applications, dependency injection testing, and model relationship testing. Provide practical examples of testing controllers, middleware, authentication flows, and API endpoints. Document test configuration options, environment-specific testing, and continuous integration setup. Address performance testing, security testing, and regression testing strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Partner Product Creation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for partner product creation workflow in KatalogThrift. Document the complete product registration process including form fields, validation rules, and data processing. Explain the product creation interface with required fields (name, brand, product_type, price, size, condition, description), optional features (story, image upload, store links), and variant configuration. Detail the validation schema covering string limits, numeric ranges, URL formats, and boolean flags. Document image upload handling for both file uploads and external URLs, size chart configuration, and slug generation. Include step-by-step examples of creating products with variants, handling size charts, and managing product metadata. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common validation errors and file upload issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search Algorithms and Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the search algorithm implementation. Document the full-text search functionality using Laravel's search scope method, keyword matching strategies, and relevance scoring mechanisms. Explain the search query processing pipeline, including query sanitization, tokenization, and fuzzy matching. Cover the integration with database full-text search capabilities and performance optimization techniques. Include examples of search query construction, result ranking algorithms, and search result pagination. Document search result formatting, highlighting matched terms, and search result caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for KatalogThrift's Laravel-based full-stack application. Describe the high-level MVC architecture, service provider registration, dependency injection container, and application bootstrap process. Document the multi-role system architecture supporting Member, Partner, and Administrator roles. Explain routing architecture including web and API routes, middleware pipeline, and request flow. Detail the application structure with clear separation of concerns between controllers, models, views, and services. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and integration patterns. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, authorization, logging, and caching. Document technology stack decisions, architectural patterns used, and scalability considerations. Provide system context diagrams showing how different components interact and data flows through the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for KatalogThrift's routing architecture covering both web and API routes. Document the route registration process, URL pattern definitions, parameter binding, and route model binding. Explain the middleware pipeline including authentication, CORS, CSRF protection, and custom middleware for role-based access control. Detail route groups, namespace organization, and resource routing patterns. Include examples of RESTful API endpoints, named routes, and URL generation. Document route caching strategies, performance optimization, and route model binding with Eloquent models. Address route constraints, validation middleware, and exception handling in routing context.

wiki_catalogs.name: Route Protection Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for KatalogThrift's role-based middleware protection system. Detail the Authenticate middleware implementation for general authentication, EnsureMemberAuthenticated for member-only routes, EnsurePartnerAuthenticated for partner-specific routes, and EnsureAdminAuthenticated for administrative access. Explain middleware execution order, guard selection logic, and redirect handling for unauthorized users. Document route model binding with authentication, middleware parameter passing, and custom middleware creation. Include practical examples of applying middleware to routes, handling authentication failures, and implementing conditional access control. Address middleware performance considerations, caching strategies, and debugging authentication middleware issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product and Partner Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Product and Partner model relationships in KatalogThrift's marketplace system. Explain how Products are associated with Partners as store owners, documenting the foreign key relationships and cascading behaviors. Detail the Product model's attributes including pricing, inventory management, size charts, color variations, and SEO metadata. Document the Partner model's store management capabilities, performance analytics, approval workflows, and commission tracking. Include examples of product listing processes, partner onboarding workflows, and inventory synchronization patterns. Explain the relationship methods, query optimization strategies for product searches, and performance considerations for marketplace queries. Document product categorization, filtering mechanisms, and the lookbook pairing functionality that connects complementary items.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Variants and Inventory Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for product variants and inventory management system. Explain variant creation, size variations, color options, and SKU management. Document inventory tracking mechanisms, stock level monitoring, and low-stock alerts. Detail bulk inventory operations, automated stock updates, and integration with external systems. Cover variant pricing strategies, cost calculations, and profit margin tracking. Include inventory forecasting, demand planning, and reorder point automation. Document barcode scanning integration, warehouse management workflows, and multi-location inventory support. Provide practical examples of variant setup, inventory reconciliation, and stock adjustment procedures with real-world scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wishlist and Favorites System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the wishlist and favorites system. Explain wishlist creation, item addition/removal, and organization workflows. Document wishlist sharing capabilities, public/private settings, and collaborative features. Detail wishlist analytics, popularity tracking, and recommendation algorithms. Include wishlist import/export functionality, bulk operations, and synchronization across devices. Address wishlist privacy controls, guest access permissions, and sharing limitations. Document wishlist item categorization, tagging, and filtering options. Provide examples of wishlist usage patterns, seasonal collections, and gift-giving features. Include troubleshooting guides for wishlist sync issues and data consistency problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Store Profile and Settings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for partner store profile management and settings configuration. Detail store profile setup, business information management, and branding customization options. Document profile editing workflows, verification status indicators, and approval requirements. Explain store settings configuration, operational hours management, and contact information updates. Include store analytics visibility settings, performance metrics configuration, and reporting preferences. Document profile photo uploads, cover image management, and visual branding options. Address store description editing, category selection, and service offerings configuration. Provide guidelines for maintaining accurate store information, best practices for profile optimization, and troubleshooting common profile management issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Moderation and Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for user moderation and management systems. Detail partner account management including approval workflows, status tracking, and performance monitoring. Document user account oversight, role management, and permission control mechanisms. Explain report handling procedures, violation detection, and automated moderation tools. Include spam prevention measures, content filtering, and community guideline enforcement. Document user verification processes, identity confirmation, and account security features. Provide practical examples of moderation workflows, decision-making criteria, and appeal processes. Address bulk user operations, account deactivation procedures, and audit trail maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.name: User-Generated Content (UGC)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for KatalogThrift's user-generated content system. Explain UGC photo submission workflows, moderation processes, and approval systems. Document the public gallery display, user contribution guidelines, and quality standards. Detail the admin moderation interface with content review, flag management, and spam prevention mechanisms. Include user submission forms, file upload restrictions, and content validation rules. Address community guidelines enforcement, user reputation systems, and content ownership considerations. Provide examples of UGC integration with social sharing, hashtag systems, and community engagement features. Document content analytics, user contribution tracking, and automated content filtering systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Catalog APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for product catalog endpoints including product listing, search, filtering, and detail retrieval. Document GET endpoints for product browse with pagination, sorting options, and category filtering. Detail search API with keyword matching, faceted search, and autocomplete functionality. Explain product detail endpoints with variant information, pricing tiers, and inventory status. Include request parameters for filtering by price range, brand, color, size, and category. Document response schemas with product metadata, images, descriptions, and related items. Provide examples of advanced search queries, bulk product retrieval, and real-time inventory updates. Address caching strategies, performance optimization, and search indexing considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tailwind CSS Styling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Tailwind CSS implementation in KatalogThrift. Document the utility-first approach, custom configuration, and design system patterns. Explain responsive design implementation, dark mode support, and component styling strategies. Cover spacing systems, color palettes, typography scales, and interactive states. Include practical examples of form styling, card layouts, navigation components, and responsive grids. Document custom utility classes, theme customization, and breakpoint management. Address performance optimization through purging unused styles, critical CSS injection, and bundle size management. Provide guidelines for maintaining design consistency and implementing design tokens.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Procedures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed deployment procedures documentation for KatalogThrift. Document production environment setup, including server requirements, PHP extensions, and database configuration. Explain environment variable management, configuration optimization, and security hardening procedures. Detail deployment workflows using various methods (manual, CI/CD, Docker). Include database migration procedures, asset compilation, and cache warming strategies. Document monitoring setup, log rotation, and performance optimization techniques. Address rollback procedures, backup strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Include troubleshooting common deployment issues, scaling considerations, and load balancing setup. Provide step-by-step deployment checklists and post-deployment verification procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Editing and Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for product editing and management capabilities in KatalogThrift. Detail the product modification workflow including field validation, data updates, and change tracking. Document the edit interface with all editable fields, conditional updates, and status modifications (active/sold/new arrival). Explain the variant management system for updating product variations, price adjustments, stock management, and condition updates. Cover image replacement procedures, URL updates for external stores, and metadata editing. Include examples of bulk updates, variant synchronization, and product deactivation. Document the slug generation for renamed products and URL structure maintenance. Provide guidance on handling concurrent edits, validation failures, and data consistency issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Filter Categories and Faceted Search
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for filter category implementation and faceted search functionality. Detail the available filter types including price range filters, size filters, brand filters, product type filters, and availability status filters. Document the filter parameter handling, URL query string management, and filter state persistence. Explain the faceted search implementation for dynamic filter generation based on product attributes. Cover filter validation, constraint enforcement, and filter combination logic. Include examples of filter API endpoints, filter UI component integration, and filter performance optimization through database indexing.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for KatalogThrift's multi-guard authentication system. Detail the role-based access control implementation supporting Member, Partner, and Administrator users. Document authentication guards configuration, middleware implementation for route protection, and session/token management. Explain password reset workflows, email verification processes, and account registration flows for each user type. Include Sanctum API authentication setup, personal access token generation, and mobile API authentication. Document login/logout processes, remember me functionality, and security measures including CSRF protection and rate limiting. Provide practical examples of implementing authentication in controllers, protecting routes, and handling authentication failures. Address common authentication scenarios, troubleshooting authentication issues, and security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Provider Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Laravel's service provider architecture in KatalogThrift. Document the service provider registration process, application bootstrapping sequence, and dependency injection container setup. Explain the role of each service provider including AppServiceProvider, AuthServiceProvider, RouteServiceProvider, and custom providers. Detail the binding process for interfaces to implementations, singleton registrations, and configuration publishing. Include examples of registering custom services, event listeners, and middleware bindings. Document the application lifecycle from provider registration to request handling, service resolution, and shutdown processes. Address provider ordering, conditional loading, and environment-specific service registrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Registration and Login Workflows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user registration and login workflows across all three user types in KatalogThrift. Document Member registration process including profile creation, email verification, and initial setup. Detail Partner registration workflow with store information, approval process, and verification requirements. Explain Administrator account creation and role assignment procedures. Cover login authentication flows, session management, remember me functionality, and logout processes. Include password reset implementation, email verification workflows, and account activation processes. Document form validation, error handling, flash messaging, and user feedback mechanisms. Address security measures including CSRF protection, rate limiting, and suspicious activity detection during authentication attempts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Outfit and Community Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for KatalogThrift's community-driven models including Outfit, OutfitItem, OutfitSave, Follower, and UgcPhoto. Detail how Outfits are collections of clothing items created by users, with OutfitItem serving as a pivot table for many-to-many relationships between Outfits and Products. Document the OutfitSave functionality for user favorites, follower relationships for community building, and UGC photo submission system. Explain the hotspot tagging system for OutfitItems, video cover support for rich media content, and share token functionality for public outfit sharing. Include examples of outfit creation workflows, community interaction patterns, and content moderation processes. Document the relationships between these models, query patterns for community feeds, and performance optimization for social features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search and Filtering System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the product search and filtering system. Detail the search algorithm implementation, keyword matching, and relevance scoring. Document filter categories including price range, size, brand, product type, and availability status. Explain faceted search functionality, auto-complete suggestions, and search history tracking. Cover advanced filtering options, custom search queries, and saved search preferences. Include search performance optimization, caching strategies, and database indexing requirements. Document search analytics, popular query tracking, and search result personalization. Provide examples of search API usage, filter implementation, and performance tuning techniques with code samples.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reviews and Ratings System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the reviews and ratings system. Detail review submission workflows, rating scales, and content moderation processes. Document review editing, deletion, and revision capabilities. Explain review aggregation algorithms, star rating calculations, and sentiment analysis. Include review verification systems, authenticity checks, and buyer-seller validation. Address review spam prevention, duplicate detection, and quality assurance measures. Document review display formatting, sorting options, and filtering capabilities. Provide examples of review engagement features, helpfulness voting, and reviewer reputation systems. Include moderation tools, reporting mechanisms, and community guideline enforcement. Address review analytics, product insights, and seller performance metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Inventory and Listings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for partner product inventory management and listing operations. Document product creation workflows, including item details entry, pricing configuration, and variant management. Explain product categorization, tagging systems, and search optimization features. Detail inventory tracking mechanisms, stock level management, and low-stock alerts. Document product modification procedures, bulk updates, and batch operations. Include product deletion processes, archiving options, and status management (active/inactive/sold). Address product media management, image upload procedures, and visual presentation options. Document product variant creation, size chart configuration, and color variations. Explain product approval workflows, quality checks, and moderation processes. Provide best practices for product photography, description writing, and inventory organization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Moderation and Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for content moderation and editorial workflow systems. Document user-generated content (UGC) moderation including photo review, approval processes, and quality standards. Explain review moderation workflows, comment filtering, and rating system oversight. Detail article and editorial content management, approval pipelines, and publishing workflows. Include automated content detection, keyword filtering, and inappropriate content identification. Document manual review processes, escalation procedures, and content removal protocols. Provide examples of moderation queues, batch processing, and content lifecycle management. Address content archiving, historical review processes, and compliance reporting features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lookbook and Outfit Curation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for KatalogThrift's lookbook and outfit curation system. Detail outfit creation workflows, item tagging, and styling recommendations. Document the lookbook display system with outfit sharing, save functionality, and social interaction features. Explain the outfit-item relationship management, product linking, and visual composition tools. Include community features like outfit voting, commenting, and sharing across social platforms. Address lookbook curation algorithms, trending outfit detection, and personalized recommendations. Document admin oversight capabilities, content moderation for outfit submissions, and quality control measures. Provide examples of outfit creation processes, styling guidelines, and community engagement best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for member user management endpoints including profile management, wishlist operations, review submission, and notification settings. Document GET/PUT endpoints for user profile retrieval and updates with validation rules and field permissions. Detail wishlist CRUD operations with add/remove item functionality and bulk management. Explain review submission endpoints with rating systems, photo uploads, and moderation workflows. Include notification preferences API with subscription management and delivery channel settings. Document follower/following endpoints for social interactions and content discovery. Provide request/response schemas for user data, privacy settings, and activity feeds. Address data protection compliance, user consent management, and GDPR requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vite Asset Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Vite's asset bundling and development workflow in KatalogThrift. Document the Vite configuration, Laravel plugin setup, and asset compilation process. Explain JavaScript module bundling, CSS processing, and hot module replacement (HMR) functionality. Cover dependency management through npm/yarn, build optimization, and production asset generation. Include practical examples of importing assets, configuring plugins, and extending the build pipeline. Document development server configuration, proxy settings, and environment-specific builds. Address asset versioning, cache busting, and CDN integration. Provide troubleshooting guides for common build issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Deletion Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for product deletion procedures in KatalogThrift. Document the complete deletion workflow including permission checks, cascade deletion handling, and data cleanup processes. Explain the deletion confirmation process, image file removal from storage, and associated variant deletion. Detail the security measures preventing unauthorized deletions and the relationship between product deletion and dependent records. Cover the deletion interface, confirmation dialogs, and success/error feedback mechanisms. Include examples of safe deletion practices, backup considerations, and data retention policies. Document the impact of product deletion on search indexing, user favorites, and analytics data. Provide troubleshooting guidance for deletion failures and orphaned record cleanup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Auto-Complete and Suggestions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the auto-complete and search suggestion system. Document the AJAX-based auto-complete implementation, minimum character threshold requirements, and suggestion result formatting. Explain the suggestion algorithm including popularity-based ranking, recent search history integration, and trending query suggestions. Cover the JSON response format for frontend consumption, suggestion caching strategies, and performance optimization for high-frequency autocomplete requests. Include examples of frontend integration patterns, debouncing techniques, and user experience enhancements for search input fields.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Models and Data Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for KatalogThrift's database schema and Eloquent models. Detail entity relationships between Users, Products, Partners, Outfits, Reviews, Wishlists, and Followers. Document field definitions, data types, constraints, and indexes for each table. Explain primary/foreign key relationships, cascading behaviors, and referential integrity rules. Include model attributes, accessors/mutators, and relationship methods. Document data validation rules, business logic constraints, and data lifecycle management. Provide database schema diagrams showing entity relationships and data flow patterns. Include sample data examples, common query patterns, and performance optimization strategies. Address data migration procedures, schema versioning, and backward compatibility considerations. Document caching strategies, indexing recommendations, and database tuning best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dependency Injection Container
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Laravel's dependency injection container and service container implementation in KatalogThrift. Explain the IoC container concept, automatic dependency resolution, and constructor injection patterns. Document service binding strategies including singleton, factory, and prototype bindings. Detail the application bootstrap process showing how the container resolves contracts to concrete implementations. Include examples of injecting dependencies into controllers, models, middleware, and services. Document the container lifecycle, resolution order, and fallback mechanisms. Address contextual binding, abstract factories, and container testing strategies. Explain how the container supports Laravel's macroable traits and service container extensions.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Authentication and Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for KatalogThrift's API authentication system using Laravel Sanctum. Detail personal access token generation, token management, and API endpoint protection. Explain token-based authentication for mobile clients, SPA integration, and third-party API access. Document token scopes, expiration handling, and refresh token mechanisms. Cover API rate limiting, token revocation, and security best practices for API authentication. Include practical examples of token creation in controllers, API endpoint authentication, and token validation middleware. Address token storage security, CORS configuration for API access, and debugging authentication issues. Document integration with frontend applications, mobile app authentication, and webhook authentication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reviews, Reports, and Notifications
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for KatalogThrift's review, reporting, and notification systems. Document the Review model for product feedback and ratings, ProductReport model for moderation and abuse reporting, Notification model for user alerts and system messages, ProductQuestion model for customer service interactions, and Testimonial model for featured customer stories. Explain the relationships between these models and their integration with the User and Product models. Detail the notification triggering mechanisms, moderation workflows, and content approval processes. Include examples of review submission processes, report handling procedures, notification delivery systems, and question-answer workflows. Document the data validation rules, spam prevention measures, and content quality controls. Explain the integration with the activity logging system and audit trail maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Media and Display
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for product media management and display systems. Explain image upload workflows, file validation, and storage optimization. Document image resizing, cropping, and thumbnail generation processes. Cover video embedding, 360-degree product views, and interactive media features. Detail SEO-friendly media handling, alt text management, and accessibility compliance. Include CDN integration, lazy loading implementation, and performance optimization techniques. Document media backup strategies, version control, and content delivery networks. Provide examples of media upload APIs, image processing pipelines, and responsive media display with practical implementation code.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notifications and Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for member notification and communication systems. Explain notification delivery mechanisms, real-time updates, and push notification capabilities. Document notification preferences, opt-in/opt-out settings, and channel selection (email, SMS, in-app). Detail notification categories, priority levels, and scheduling options. Include notification analytics, open rates, click-through rates, and engagement metrics. Address notification customization, template management, and personalization features. Document notification history, archive management, and bulk operations. Provide examples of automated notification triggers, subscription management, and promotional messaging. Include troubleshooting guides for notification delivery failures and user preference conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics and Performance Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for partner analytics dashboard and performance tracking systems. Detail key performance indicators (KPIs), sales metrics, and revenue tracking features. Document visitor analytics, traffic sources, and conversion rate monitoring. Explain product performance metrics, best-selling items tracking, and inventory turnover analysis. Include customer acquisition costs, lifetime value calculations, and marketing effectiveness measurements. Document real-time performance monitoring, automated reporting features, and data export capabilities. Address trend analysis, seasonal performance patterns, and growth tracking. Explain competitor benchmarking, market positioning insights, and industry comparison features. Provide guidance on interpreting analytics data, identifying performance trends, and making data-driven business decisions. Include troubleshooting analytics issues and optimizing dashboard configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Administration Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for system administration and operational tools. Detail administrative interface configuration, permission management, and access control mechanisms. Document system monitoring tools, performance metrics collection, and health check implementations. Explain notification system administration, broadcast messaging, and communication channel management. Include system maintenance procedures, backup operations, and disaster recovery protocols. Document logging and auditing systems, activity tracking, and compliance reporting features. Provide examples of administrative workflows, system configuration management, and operational troubleshooting procedures. Address security hardening, vulnerability assessment, and system optimization recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Community and Social Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for KatalogThrift's community and social interaction features. Explain follower/following system implementation, social graph management, and user relationship tracking. Document community discovery features, user recommendation algorithms, and network effects. Detail social sharing capabilities, content amplification, and community engagement metrics. Include privacy controls for social interactions, blocking mechanisms, and community guidelines enforcement. Address community analytics, user activity tracking, and engagement optimization strategies. Provide examples of community building workflows, social proof mechanisms, and user retention features. Document integration with external social platforms and cross-platform identity management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Partner Integration APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for partner store owner integration endpoints including product management, outfit creation, and analytics reporting. Document product CRUD endpoints with inventory synchronization, pricing management, and variant handling. Detail outfit creation APIs with item association, styling guidance, and lookbook publishing workflows. Explain analytics endpoints providing sales metrics, customer insights, and performance reports. Include inventory sync APIs with real-time stock updates and automated restocking alerts. Document order management integration with fulfillment tracking and shipping coordination. Provide examples of webhook implementations for inventory changes, order confirmations, and promotional campaigns. Address partner onboarding APIs, store approval workflows, and compliance verification processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: JavaScript Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for JavaScript integration and frontend interactivity in KatalogThrift. Document the application's JavaScript architecture, ES6 modules, and library integration patterns. Explain AJAX interactions with Laravel backend, form handling, and dynamic content loading. Cover DOM manipulation, event handling, and user interface enhancements. Include practical examples of API communication, real-time updates, and interactive components. Document the integration between Blade templates and JavaScript functionality, including data passing and component initialization. Address browser compatibility, polyfills, and progressive enhancement strategies. Provide guidelines for state management, error handling, and performance optimization in client-side code.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Product Moderation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for administrative product moderation in KatalogThrift. Detail the admin review workflow for new product listings, quality assurance processes, and approval procedures. Document the moderation interface with product listing, filtering options, and bulk actions. Explain the approval criteria, content verification standards, and policy enforcement mechanisms. Cover the review process for flagged products, user reports, and automated content screening. Include examples of product approval, rejection workflows, and publisher notifications. Document the audit trail for moderation actions, appeal processes, and quality metrics tracking. Provide guidance on handling edge cases, policy violations, and community guideline enforcement.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search Performance and Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for search performance optimization and database efficiency. Detail database indexing strategies for search columns, full-text search optimization, and query performance tuning. Document caching mechanisms for frequently searched terms, search result caching, and CDN optimization for product images. Explain database query optimization techniques, eager loading strategies, and pagination performance considerations. Cover search analytics implementation, popular query tracking, and search volume monitoring. Include benchmarking methodologies, performance metrics collection, and optimization best practices for large-scale product catalogs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Catalog Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for KatalogThrift's product catalog management system. Thoroughly explain product creation, editing, and deletion workflows for partners. Document product variants, size charts, pricing strategies, and inventory management. Include product categorization, tagging, and SEO optimization features. Explain search functionality, filtering mechanisms, and product discovery algorithms. Detail partner product approval workflows, moderation processes, and quality control measures. Document product media management, image optimization, and CDN integration. Include bulk product operations, import/export capabilities, and API endpoints for product data. Address product analytics, performance metrics, and sales tracking. Provide practical examples of product CRUD operations, variant management, and search implementation. Document common product-related issues and their solutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware Pipeline Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for KatalogThrift's middleware pipeline architecture. Document the global middleware stack, route middleware registration, and middleware ordering in the kernel. Explain the authentication middleware chain including role-based access control for Member, Partner, and Administrator users. Detail custom middleware implementation patterns, request/response modification, and early termination scenarios. Include examples of middleware execution flow, parameter passing, and conditional middleware application. Document middleware grouping strategies, priority handling, and performance considerations. Address middleware testing approaches, exception handling within middleware, and debugging middleware chains. Explain how middleware integrates with Laravel's event system and request lifecycle.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Measures and Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for KatalogThrift's authentication security measures and best practices. Document CSRF protection implementation, maintenance mode handling, and session security configurations. Explain password security policies, hash algorithms, and secure session management. Detail rate limiting mechanisms, brute force protection, and suspicious activity detection. Cover secure cookie configuration, HTTPS enforcement, and security headers implementation. Include practical examples of implementing security middleware, handling authentication errors securely, and logging authentication events. Address common security vulnerabilities in authentication systems, penetration testing considerations, and incident response procedures for authentication-related security issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interactions and Wishlist Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for KatalogThrift's user interaction models including Wishlist management, UserBadge system, and ActivityLog tracking. Detail how users manage favorite products through the Wishlist model, the gamification system with UserBadge achievements and point-based rewards, and the comprehensive ActivityLog for tracking user actions and system events. Explain the relationship between wishlists and product recommendations, badge earning criteria and progression systems, and activity tracking for behavioral analytics. Include examples of wishlist operations, badge acquisition workflows, and activity log generation for audit trails. Document the integration with the user points system, tier progression mechanics, and personalized recommendation algorithms. Explain data retention policies, privacy considerations, and performance optimization for large-scale user interaction tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Analytics and Performance Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for product analytics and performance metrics system. Detail conversion rate tracking, sales funnel analysis, and revenue attribution models. Document product popularity metrics, view-to-purchase ratios, and customer engagement indicators. Explain inventory turnover analysis, slow-moving stock identification, and markdown optimization. Cover A/B testing capabilities, product performance comparisons, and seasonal trend analysis. Include real-time analytics dashboards, custom report generation, and export functionality. Document data collection mechanisms, privacy compliance, and analytics API endpoints. Provide examples of analytics implementation, custom metric calculation, and performance monitoring with actionable insights.

wiki_catalogs.name: Community and Social Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for member community and social features. Detail outfit saving, sharing, and community showcase functionality. Document follower/following systems, social connections, and network building. Explain community guidelines, content standards, and moderation tools. Address community growth strategies, user acquisition through social proof, and retention through community engagement.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# KatalogThrift: Multi-Mitra Thrift Fashion Platform

## Project Introduction

**KatalogThrift** is a comprehensive Laravel-based e-commerce platform designed specifically for secondhand fashion, focusing on thrifted hoodies and streetwear items. The platform operates as a multi-mitra (multi-partner) marketplace that connects individual thrift stores across Indonesia with fashion-conscious consumers seeking quality pre-owned clothing.

### Purpose Statement
The platform aims to revolutionize the thrift shopping experience by combining traditional brick-and-mortar thrift stores with modern digital commerce, creating a curated marketplace that promotes sustainable fashion consumption while supporting local thrift businesses.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Sustainable Fashion**: Promote circular economy principles by encouraging secondhand clothing consumption
- **Local Business Support**: Empower thrift store owners across Indonesia through digital marketplace access
- **Curated Experience**: Provide high-quality, well-maintained thrift items with detailed product information
- **Community Building**: Foster engagement between consumers, thrift stores, and fashion enthusiasts

### Target Audience
- **Primary**: Fashion-conscious individuals aged 18-35 seeking unique, quality thrift pieces
- **Secondary**: Local thrift store owners looking to expand their customer base digitally
- **Tertiary**: Sustainability advocates interested in reducing fashion waste

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern Laravel architecture with clear separation of concerns and role-based access control:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Web Routes]
    B --> C[HTTP Controllers]
    C --> D[Business Logic]
    D --> E[Eloquent Models]
    E --> F[(MySQL Database)]
    
    G[API Routes] --> H[Sanctum Auth]
    H --> I[API Controllers]
    I --> D
    
    J[Blade Templates] --> K[Frontend Assets]
    K --> L[Vite Build System]
    
    M[Middleware Layer] --> N[Authentication]
    N --> O[Authorization]
    O --> P[Role-based Access Control]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Architecture Layers:**
- **Presentation Layer**: Blade templates with Tailwind CSS styling
- **Application Layer**: Laravel controllers handling business logic
- **Domain Layer**: Eloquent models representing business entities
- **Infrastructure Layer**: Database connections and caching systems

**Design Patterns:**
- **Repository Pattern**: Through Eloquent models and query builders
- **Factory Pattern**: For model creation and seeding
- **Observer Pattern**: Through model events and activity logging
- **Strategy Pattern**: For payment processing and shipping methods

### System Relationships

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "User Roles"
        A[Member Users]
        B[Partner Store Owners]
        C[Administrators]
    end
    
    subgraph "Core Modules"
        D[Product Catalog]
        E[Store Management]
        F[Order Processing]
        G[Content Management]
        H[Analytics Dashboard]
    end
    
    subgraph "Support Systems"
        I[User Authentication]
        J[Payment Processing]
        K[Inventory Management]
        L[Notification System]
    end
    
    A --> D
    B --> E
    C --> H
    D --> K
    E --> K
    F --> J
    G --> L
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Web Routes**: `/routes/web.php` - Primary application routing with role-based middleware
- **API Routes**: `/routes/api.php` - RESTful API endpoints with Sanctum authentication
- **Application Bootstrap**: `/bootstrap/app.php` - Service container initialization

### Core Modules

**Business Logic Controllers:**
- **CatalogController**: Product browsing, filtering, and product detail management
- **MemberAuthController**: User registration, authentication, and password management
- **PartnerDashboardController**: Store owner management interface
- **AdminDashboardController**: Platform administration and analytics

**Data Models:**
- **Product Model**: E-commerce product management with variants and inventory tracking
- **Partner Model**: Store owner profiles with performance metrics and verification status
- **User Model**: Role-based authentication with gamification features
- **Review Model**: Customer feedback and rating system

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration:**
- **Database Configuration**: Multi-connection support for MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite
- **Catalog Settings**: Brand identity, product categories, and social media integration
- **Authentication Guards**: Separate guards for member, partner, and admin users
- **Storage Configuration**: Cloud storage integration for product images and documents

**Build System:**
- **Vite Configuration**: Modern asset bundling with hot module replacement
- **Frontend Dependencies**: Axios for HTTP requests, Laravel Vite plugin integration

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies:**
- **Laravel Framework**: ^10.10 - Application foundation and ecosystem
- **Laravel Sanctum**: ^3.3 - API authentication and personal access tokens
- **Guzzle HTTP**: ^7.2 - HTTP client for external service communication

**Development Tools:**
- **Vite**: ^5.0.0 - Modern build tool for frontend assets
- **Axios**: ^1.6.4 - Promise-based HTTP client
- **Testing Framework**: PHPUnit for automated testing

### Integration Points

**Third-Party Services:**
- **Social Media Integration**: Instagram, TikTok, Shopee, Tokopedia connectivity
- **Payment Processing**: Modular payment gateway architecture
- **Storage Services**: Cloud storage for product images and user uploads
- **Analytics Integration**: Performance monitoring and user behavior tracking

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class User {
        +string name
        +string email
        +string role
        +isAdmin() bool
        +isPartner() bool
        +isMember() bool
    }
    
    class Product {
        +string slug
        +string name
        +float price
        +boolean isActive
        +partner() Partner
        +reviews() Review[]
        +variants() ProductVariant[]
    }
    
    class Partner {
        +string storeName
        +string status
        +user() User
        +products() Product[]
    }
    
    class Review {
        +integer rating
        +string comment
        +user() User
        +product() Product
    }
    
    User --> Partner : owns
    User --> Review : writes
    Product --> Review : receives
    Partner --> Product : manages
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Role User System:**
- **Member Users**: Browse products, manage wishlists, write reviews, follow stores
- **Partner Stores**: Manage inventory, handle orders, track analytics, upload products
- **Administrators**: Platform moderation, user management, content curation

**E-commerce Capabilities:**
- Advanced product filtering by category, brand, price, and availability
- Product variant management with size charts and color variations
- Shopping cart functionality with real-time inventory updates
- Secure checkout process with multiple payment options

**Content Management:**
- Editorial articles and fashion guides
- Lookbook curation with interactive outfit displays
- User-generated content (UGC) submission and moderation
- Social media integration and sharing capabilities

### Implementation Highlights

**Product Management System:**
- `/app/Models/Product.php` - Comprehensive product model with SEO optimization
- `/app/Http/Controllers/CatalogController.php` - Dynamic product catalog with advanced filtering
- `/config/catalog.php` - Centralized brand configuration and product type definitions

**User Authentication Flow:**
- `/app/Http/Controllers/Member/MemberAuthController.php` - Complete authentication lifecycle
- `/app/Http/Middleware/Authenticate.php` - Custom authentication middleware
- `/config/auth.php` - Multi-guard authentication configuration

**Store Owner Portal:**
- `/app/Http/Controllers/Partner/` - Complete partner management interface
- `/resources/views/partner/` - Partner-specific frontend templates
- `/app/Models/Partner.php` - Store owner profile management with performance analytics

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Guest
    Guest --> MemberRegistration: Register
    Guest --> MemberLogin: Login
    MemberRegistration --> MemberDashboard
    MemberLogin --> MemberDashboard
    MemberDashboard --> ProductBrowsing
    MemberDashboard --> StoreManagement: Partner Account
    MemberDashboard --> AdminAccess: Admin Account
    
    state ProductBrowsing {
        [*] --> CategorySelection
        CategorySelection --> FilterProducts
        FilterProducts --> ProductDetail
        ProductDetail --> AddToCart
        AddToCart --> Checkout
    }
    
    state StoreManagement {
        [*] --> ProductListing
        ProductListing --> InventoryUpdate
        InventoryUpdate --> AnalyticsView
    }
    
    state AdminAccess {
        [*] --> UserManagement
        UserManagement --> ContentModeration
        ContentModeration --> SystemAnalytics
    }
```

**Performance Considerations:**
- Database indexing for product search and filtering operations
- Image optimization and lazy loading for improved page load times
- Caching strategies for frequently accessed product data
- CDN integration for global content delivery

**Security Measures:**
- CSRF protection and input validation throughout the application
- Role-based access control preventing unauthorized administrative actions
- Secure password hashing and session management
- API rate limiting and request throttling

The platform represents a sophisticated blend of e-commerce functionality, community building features, and sustainable business practices, positioned to become a leading destination for conscious fashion consumption in the Indonesian market.

---

**Sources:**
- [composer.json](composer.json)
- [package.json](package.json)
- [routes/web.php](routes/web.php)
- [routes/api.php](routes/api.php)
- [app/Models/Product.php](app/Models/Product.php)
- [app/Models/Partner.php](app/Models/Partner.php)
- [app/Http/Controllers/CatalogController.php](app/Http/Controllers/CatalogController.php)
- [config/catalog.php](config/catalog.php)
- [config/database.php](config/database.php)
- [app/Http/Controllers/Member/MemberAuthController.php](app/Http/Controllers/Member/MemberAuthController.php)
- [resources/views/welcome.blade.php](resources/views/welcome.blade.php)
- [app/Http/Middleware/Authenticate.php](app/Http/Middleware/Authenticate.php)
- [config/auth.php](config/auth.php)
- [app/Models/User.php](app/Models/User.php)
- [vite.config.js](vite.config.js)

</blog>
